parsed_review.summary: CP00-537 is a descriptor-only AI-governance closeout pack covering 40 RP17 units (RP17.P06.M03.S09-RP17.P06.M04.S23: 17 Primary Implementation Slice + 23 Secondary Workflow Slice rows). Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack binding is consistent across registry.js, contracts/ai-governance-core-contract.json, docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json, the cp00-537 manifest snapshot, and review-baseline.json (40 units, risk B, upstream CP00-536, next CP00-538/RP17.P06.M04.S24). Deliverable distribution (implementation 11, ui 8, claude_review 3, security_audit 8, test 8, hermes_evidence 2) and micro-title distribution (17/23) match exactly. All no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced (freezeCp537Result forces every runtime/write/audit/leak flag false; synthetic_only true; loads_real_fixture_data false). The pack-level production_ready claim is false with runtime_ready false and implementation_layer descriptor, so no production gate is weakened; the Hermes production_ready_candidate flag is scoped strictly to descriptor coverage/slice validation. Claude authority boundary is preserved (claude_is_final_approval false, human final approval required, no enterprise-trust claim from local validation) and asserted by both tests and the contract validator. Hermes patterns are used only for descriptor-only review packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false). Review hardening is in place: forbidden_review_evidence rejects failed auth, zero-byte stdout, malformed JSON, fenced JSON, and tool-call-shaped output; the recorded auth preflight passed with valid 1445-byte JSON. Changed-file-scope correctly isolates unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room UI, workbook docs) from the 8 in-scope refs. No P0-P3 defects found.
